Use the knowledge above if relevant to answer:
odoo module structure

Now I have a clear picture. Here's a simple explanation of the **Odoo module structure**:

---

## Odoo Module Structure

An Odoo module is simply a **folder** with a specific set of files. Here's the standard layout using the `custom_sale` module as an example:

```
custom_sale/                          ← Module folder (name = technical module name)
│
├── __init__.py                       ← Tells Python this is a package; imports sub-folders
├── __manifest__.py                   ← Module info: name, version, dependencies, files to load
├── pyproject.toml                    ← Optional Python packaging info
├── README.rst                        ← Optional description
│
├── models/                           ← Business logic (Python files)
│   ├── __init__.py                   ← Imports all model files
│   ├── res_partner.py                ← Custom fields/logic for Partners
│   └── sale_order.py                 ← Custom fields/logic for Sales Orders
│
├── views/                            ← User interface (XML files)
│   └── templates.xml                 ← Form views, list views, website templates
│
├── controllers/                      ← Website/page controllers (optional)
│   ├── __init__.py
│   └── main.py
│
├── data/                             ← Default data to load (optional)
│   └── theme.ir.attachment.csv
│
├── security/                         ← Access rights & rules (optional)
│   └── ir.model.access.csv
│
├── static/                           ← Images, CSS, JavaScript files
│   └── description/
│       └── icon.png                  ← Module icon (shown in Apps menu)
│
└── report/                           ← Custom reports (optional)
```

### What each key file does:

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **`__manifest__.py`** | The module's "ID card" — contains name, version, list of dependencies (`depends`), and which XML/data files to load (`data`) |
| **`__init__.py`** | Just imports the sub-folders so Python loads them |
| **`models/*.py`** | Your business logic — add new fields, override existing methods, etc. |
| **`views/*.xml`** | Your user interface — add fields to forms, create new screens, modify website pages |
| **`controllers/*.py`** | Handle custom website URLs and page routing |

### How it all connects:

1. Odoo reads **`__manifest__.py`** to know what the module is and what files to load
2. It loads **`__init__.py`** which imports the **`models/`** folder
3. The models folder registers new fields and logic in the database
4. The **`views/`** XML files tell Odoo how to display those fields on screen
5. When you **upgrade** the module, Odoo applies all these changes to the database
